name: Do I need to move out while my new floors are being installed?
headline: Do I need to move out while my new floors are being installed?

mainEntity.name: Do I need to move out while my new floors are being installed?
mainEntity.text: Do I need to move out while my new floors are being installed?

acceptedAnswer.text: You usually do not need to leave your home during flooring installation, but you should be prepared for noise, dust, and limited access to certain rooms. For floating floors like laminate or vinyl plank in a few rooms, many families stay in the home and simply avoid the work areas. It can be loud, and there will be some dust and odors from cutting and adhesives, but it is manageable for most people. With larger projects, especially sanding and finishing hardwood or cutting a lot of tile, the noise, dust, and fumes can be more intense. Oil-based finishes in particular can have strong smells for a day or two. If anyone in the home has asthma, allergies, or is very sensitive to odors, staying elsewhere during the dustiest or smelliest steps may be more comfortable. Plan ahead by setting up temporary sleeping and living areas away from the work zone, if possible. Talk to your installer about their schedule and which areas will be blocked off each day. Ask what they will do for dust control, such as plastic barriers or vacuum attachments. One pitfall is underestimating how disruptive the process can be, especially in smaller homes. It is better to plan for some inconvenience and have a backup place to stay, even if you end up not needing it.
